title: Safety Management System (SMS)
summary: How Glidepath implements the four AC 150/5200-37A SMS pillars — hazard register, 5×5 risk matrix, SPIs, Management of Change, and the public anonymous safety-report form.
concept:
The SMS module is gated exclusively to civilian FAA Part 139 bases (bases.airport_type === 'faa_part139') and is invisible to USAF installations. The mode source of truth is lib/airport-mode.ts; the module-config layer enforces the gate via appliesTo: ['faa_part139'].

The four AC 150/5200-37A pillars

  • Safety Policy — a versioned sms_policies row signed by the Accountable Executive (AE). New policies start as drafts; activation is atomic via the sign_sms_policy SECURITY DEFINER RPC, which also supersedes the prior active row.
  • Safety Risk Management — the hazard register (sms_hazards), per-hazard risk assessments (sms_risk_assessments), and mitigations (sms_mitigations). Every hazard gets a minted hazard_code (e.g. HZ-0042) via the _sms_next_code RPC. Risk scoring is a 5×5 matrix: risk_index = likelihood × severity; the band classifier in lib/supabase/sms.ts maps index ≥ 15 → high, ≥ 7 → medium, else low.
  • Safety Assurance — Safety Performance Indicators (sms_spis) with scheduled measurements, audits (sms_audits), and Management of Change (sms_management_of_change). SPIs are seeded idempotently on first hazard creation via _sms_seed_default_spis. MoC approval/rejection goes through approve_sms_moc / reject_sms_moc RPCs so the audit trail is atomic.
  • Safety Promotion — safety reports (sms_safety_reports) and communications (sms_communications). Anonymous public reports arrive via the submit_safety_report_public SECURITY DEFINER RPC (no auth required). Staff can promote a report directly to a hazard via promote_safety_report_to_hazard.

AE dashboard (the landing page)

The four hero cards at /sms aggregate data from fetchAeSummary(), which calls six sub-fetches in parallel and reduces them into a single SmsAeSummary object. The dashboard drives the risk band display using the BAND_COLORS constant (shared between UI and the BandChip component).

Public safety reporting endpoint

The anonymous form lives at /[icao]/sms-report (a public, unauthenticated route). It resolves the base by ICAO via get_public_safety_report_config_by_icao, checks that the SMS module is enabled on that base, and submits via submit_safety_report_public. Reporter contact fields are optional and surfaced only to SMS triagers via RLS. Per 14 CFR §139.401(c)(2), good-faith reports are non-retributive — the form copy states this explicitly.

Risk matrix UI

The RiskMatrix component (components/sms/risk-matrix.tsx) renders a 5×5 interactive grid. Each cell shows its computed risk_index value. Optional current and residual props place before/after markers so assessors see the mitigation effect at a glance. Passing onPick makes the grid clickable for assessment entry — faster than two separate dropdowns.

    policyReviewDueDate: policy?.review_due_date ?? null,
    hazardsByBand: bandCounts,

fits: All SMS data functions are in lib/supabase/sms.ts. Pages live under app/(app)/sms/. The risk matrix and band chip are shared components in components/sms/. The public form is at app/[icao]/sms-report/page.tsx. The full SMS Manual PDF export is generated by lib/sms-pdf.ts. All writes log to activity_log via logActivity() and errors surface through friendlyError().

quiz.q: A new risk assessment is created with likelihood=4 and severity=3. What risk_band does classifyRiskBand() return?
choices: low — index 12 is below the medium threshold of 15
medium — index 12 is between 7 and 14
high — index 12 exceeds the high threshold
unassessed — only the DB trigger sets the band
quiz.explain: classifyRiskBand multiplies likelihood × severity: 4 × 3 = 12. The thresholds are: ≥ 15 → high, ≥ 7 → medium, else low. 12 is between 7 and 14, so the result is 'medium'.

quiz.q: When an Accountable Executive clicks 'Sign Policy' in the UI, which function performs the actual database transition?
choices: updateDraftPolicy() — patches the row status to 'active'
signPolicy() — calls the sign_sms_policy SECURITY DEFINER RPC
createDraftPolicy() — inserts a new active row directly
approveMoc() — the MoC approval path also covers policy sign-off
quiz.explain: signPolicy() in lib/supabase/sms.ts calls the sign_sms_policy SECURITY DEFINER RPC, which atomically sets the draft to 'active' and supersedes any prior active policy. A direct status UPDATE is intentionally avoided because the supersede step must be atomic.

quiz.q: A user submits an anonymous safety report via the public /[icao]/sms-report page. Which Supabase mechanism allows this without user authentication?
choices: RLS policy with USING (true) on sms_safety_reports INSERT
A SECURITY DEFINER RPC (submit_safety_report_public) called from the anon role
A Next.js server action that uses a service-role client
A public Supabase Edge Function that bypasses RLS
quiz.explain: The public form calls submit_safety_report_public via RPC. SECURITY DEFINER RPCs run with the definer's privileges rather than the caller's, so they can insert into tables that the anon role otherwise cannot touch. The component in public-safety-report-form.tsx calls (supabase as any).rpc('submit_safety_report_public', {...}) with the anon key — no cookie auth is needed.

title: Airport Emergency Plan (AEP)
summary: How Glidepath manages the 14 CFR §139.325 AEP lifecycle — plan supersession, agency roster, comms checks, drills, and the automated annual-review digest cron.
concept:
The AEP module is gated to civilian FAA Part 139 bases only (bases.airport_type === 'faa_part139'). USAF bases use the Secondary Crash Net (SCN) module instead. The gate is enforced in the module-config layer via appliesTo: ['faa_part139']; the isCivilian() helper in lib/airport-mode.ts is the runtime check.

Five supporting tables

TablePurpose
aep_plansVersioned plan documents with FAA acceptance fields + AE sign-off
aep_response_agenciesRoster grouped by role (ARFF, EMS, police, ATC, FAA RO, NTSB…)
aep_drills§139.325(h/j) drill log — full-scale, tabletop, functional, etc.
aep_comms_checksPer-cycle comms-check rollup with started/completed timestamps
aep_comms_check_resultsPer-agency status snapshot for each comms check

Plan supersession (not UPDATE)

Uploading a new AEP version calls supersedePlan(). The old plan's replaced_by_id column is back-filled to the new plan's ID; the new row has replaced_by_id IS NULL. The active plan is always the single row where replaced_by_id IS NULL. This is the same append-only supersede pattern used by field condition reports — never a destructive UPDATE on historical compliance records.

Annual review cadence

nextAnnualReviewDue() is a pure function in lib/supabase/aep.ts. It anchors off last_reviewed_at ?? effective_date and adds one year. The 60-day amber window (vs the 180-day window for full-scale drills) drives the dashboard card color. The function is tested in tests/aep.test.ts for the midnight-UTC truncation edge case (same pattern as daysToExpiry in the training module).

Full-scale drill cadence

nextFullScaleDue() computes the triennial (36 calendar-month) due date from the most-recent completed full-scale drill. Status values: never / overdue / due_soon (within 180 days) / current.

Automated annual-review digest

The Vercel cron at app/api/annual-review-digest/route.ts runs at 13:30 UTC daily. It queries all civilian bases, classifies their AEP and WHMP review status, and sends a per-base digest email via Resend when either review is inside the 60-day amber window or already overdue. A dedup row in annual_review_digest_log (unique on (base_id, send_date)) prevents duplicate sends on re-runs. The cron is gated by CRON_SECRET and uses a service-role Supabase client (no cookie auth).

SMS feed integration

Completed full-scale drills and completed comms checks automatically feed the SMS Safety Performance Indicators on the next nightly SPI recompute (02:30 UTC). The AEP dashboard displays a reminder callout linking to /sms/spis.

code.caption: nextAnnualReviewDue and nextFullScaleDue — pure date-math functions that drive dashboard chips and the digest cron
summarizeCommsCheck — builds a human-readable summary string for the activity log entry on check completion
code.to:
return `AEP comms check complete — all loud & clear except ${parts.join(', ')}`
}

fits: Data layer: lib/supabase/aep.ts. PDF generators: lib/aep-pdf.ts (plan PDF, drill log PDF, monthly comms PDF). Pages: app/(app)/aep/ (dashboard, plan, agencies, comms-checks, drills). Annual-review cron: app/api/annual-review-digest/route.ts. The shared date helper used by both AEP and the training digest is lib/annual-review-due.ts.

quiz.q: The airport operator uploads a new AEP version. What happens to the old plan row in the database?
choices: It is deleted — only one plan row is kept per base
Its status column is set to 'superseded'
Its replaced_by_id column is back-filled with the new plan's UUID
It is archived to a separate aep_plan_history table
quiz.explain: supersedePlan() back-fills replaced_by_id on the old row. The active plan is always identified by replaced_by_id IS NULL — no status column toggle and no separate history table. History is the same table, queried with fetchPlanHistory().

quiz.q: nextAnnualReviewDue() returns status='due_soon'. What does that mean in terms of calendar days?
choices: The review is overdue — daysOut is negative
The review is within 180 days of the due date
The review is within 60 days of the due date
The review is within 30 days of the due date
quiz.explain: The AEP annual review uses a 60-day amber window (compared to the 180-day window for full-scale drills). In nextAnnualReviewDue(), 'due_soon' fires when daysOut <= 60. The threshold is consistent with the annual-review-digest cron's REVIEW_WARNING_WINDOW_DAYS constant.

quiz.q: The annual-review digest cron runs twice in the same day due to a Vercel retry. What prevents a duplicate email from being sent?
choices: An in-memory cache keyed on base_id in the route handler module
A unique constraint on (base_id, send_date) in annual_review_digest_log — the second INSERT returns a 23505 conflict code and the send is skipped
The cron checks the Resend delivery log before sending
A Redis distributed lock with a 24-hour TTL
quiz.explain: The cron inserts into annual_review_digest_log before each send. The table has a unique constraint on (base_id, send_date). If the INSERT returns error code 23505 (unique_violation), the handler skips the send entirely for that base. Vercel serverless can't use in-memory caches across invocations, so the dedup must live in Postgres.

title: §139.303 Training
summary: How Glidepath tracks the 14 CFR Part 139 §139.303(e) 13-topic training curriculum, stores completions with computed expiry, and sends the daily 30-day-warning digest cron.
concept:
The §139.303 Training module is gated to civilian FAA Part 139 bases (bases.airport_type === 'faa_part139'). The module-config key is appliesTo: ['faa_part139']. Note that the URL slug /training is held by this civilian module; USAF training lives under /amtr (Airfield Management Training Record). The in-app help/guide page for the platform lives at /help.

Four supporting tables

TablePurpose
training_topicsSystem seed (base_id NULL) + base-custom rows. System rows cover the 13 §139.303(e) topics. Bases can override recurrency frequency or add custom topics without touching system rows.
training_recordsAppend-only completion log. Each row stores completed_at, training_type (initial / recurrent / remedial), and a materialized expires_at set by a DB trigger from the topic's recurrent_frequency_months.
training_renewalsExplicit supersession chains — links a previous record to its renewal record for audit trail continuity.
training_certificatesProfessional credentials (AAAE-CM, ACE-Ops, ACE-Comm, ACE-Sec, ACE-WHC) with issued/expires dates.

Status classifier

classifyTrainingStatus() is a pure function that accepts the most-recent record for a (user, topic) pair and returns 'current' / 'expiring' / 'expired' / 'not_started'. Thresholds: more than 90 days to expiry → current (green); 30–90 days → expiring (amber); fewer than 30 days or already past → expired (red). A record with expires_at == null (no recurrence cadence) is always current.

daysToExpiry precision

daysToExpiry() truncates both sides to midnight UTC before computing the delta, preventing a half-day rounding error when a record expires at noon and is read in the afternoon. The same midnight-truncation pattern appears in daysBetween() in lib/supabase/aep.ts. This precision matters when the cron fires: a record expiring at 2027-05-26T23:59:59Z read at 13:00 UTC on 2027-05-26 should return 0, not −1.

Training expiry digest cron

The cron at app/api/training-expiry-digest/route.ts fires at 13:00 UTC daily. It queries all training_records where expires_at falls within the next 30 days on FAA Part 139 bases, drops any row superseded by a later renewal for the same (user, topic), groups the remainder by base, and sends one digest email per base to the training admin recipient via Resend. Dedup is via a training_digest_log table (same pattern as the annual-review digest). Auth: Bearer CRON_SECRET in the Authorization header; a service-role Supabase client is used so no cookie auth is needed.

System topics vs. base topics

fetchTrainingTopics() fetches rows where base_id IS NULL (system seeds) OR base_id = current base. A base can override a system topic by calling createTopicOverride(), which clones the system row with a base-specific base_id and the desired frequency/retention changes. The system row is unchanged and continues to seed all other bases.

code.caption: classifyTrainingStatus and daysToExpiry — the pure status classifier shared by roster, compliance matrix, sidebar badge, and the digest cron
fetchTrainingTopics — fetches system (base_id NULL) and base-custom topics in display order
code.from: export async function fetchTrainingTopics(baseId: string): Promise<TrainingTopic[]> {

code.caption: createTopicOverride — clones a system topic row as a base-specific override without touching the system row
Training expiry digest cron — queries expiring records within 30 days, drops superseded renewals, sends per-base digest

fits: Data layer: lib/supabase/training-part139.ts. Module definitions (topic titles, roles, apply-to arrays): lib/training/modules.ts. PDF export: lib/training-part139-pdf.ts. Pages: app/(app)/training/ (landing, topics, roster, compliance, [userId]). Expiry cron: app/api/training-expiry-digest/route.ts. The status classifier is consumed by the roster page, the compliance matrix, and the sidebar badge count hook.

quiz.q: A training record has expires_at = '2026-07-15' and today is 2026-06-01. What status does classifyTrainingStatus() return?
choices: current — more than 90 days remaining
expiring — between 30 and 90 days remaining
expired — fewer than 30 days or already past
not_started — no record was provided
quiz.explain: June 1 to July 15 is 44 days. The thresholds are: > 90 days → current, 30–90 days → expiring, < 30 days or past → expired. 44 days falls between 30 and 90, so the status is 'expiring' (amber).

quiz.q: A base wants a different recurrence interval for topic '139.303(e)(5)' than the 12-month system default. What is the correct approach in the codebase?
choices: UPDATE the system topic row directly to change recurrent_frequency_months
Call createTopicOverride() to clone the system row with a base_id and the new frequency
Delete the system row and recreate it with the desired frequency for this base
Set an override flag on the training_records row at completion time
quiz.explain: createTopicOverride() clones the system row (base_id NULL) as a new row with the current base's ID and the override values. fetchTrainingTopics() then returns the base-specific row instead of the system row, because both base_id=null AND base_id=current are fetched and the base row takes precedence by display order. The system row is untouched and continues to serve all other bases.

quiz.q: The training-expiry digest cron queries records expiring within 30 days but then does an additional in-memory filter before sending. What does that filter remove?
choices: Records belonging to USAF bases that accidentally matched the query
Records where a later training_record for the same (user, topic) already exists — i.e. records that have been superseded by a renewal
Records where the user's profile has been deleted (ON DELETE SET NULL profiles FK)
Records older than 24 months that should no longer be counted per the retention requirement
quiz.explain: The DB query fetches all records expiring in the next 30 days, but it's possible for a user to have two records on the same topic — the old expiring one and a newer renewal. The in-memory filter drops any expiring record that has been superseded (a newer completed_at exists for the same user_id + topic_id combination) so only genuinely upcoming expirations trigger the digest email.

title: Field Conditions / TALPA
summary: How Glidepath implements the AC 150/5200-30D TALPA framework — append-only per-third RwyCC computation, materialized FICON NOTAM text, and the supersede-chain that keeps the active report per runway always current.
concept:
The Field Conditions module is gated to civilian FAA Part 139 bases (bases.airport_type === 'faa_part139') via appliesTo: ['faa_part139'] in the module config. The regulatory authority is 14 CFR §139.313 and AC 150/5200-30D (TALPA — Takeoff and Landing Performance Assessment).

Append-only design — never UPDATE a report

Field condition reports are never edited in place. Every revision issues a new report row that supersedes the prior one. The superseded_by_id column on the old row is back-filled after the new row is inserted. The currently active report per runway is the row where superseded_by_id IS NULL and valid_until > now(). This ensures the audit trail is immutable — historical runway conditions remain queryable. The deleteReport() function exists only for pre-publication drafts that have not yet been copied to the FAA NOTAM Manager.

Two database tables

TableContents
field_condition_reportsOne row per issued report: base, runway, generated timestamp, valid_until, temperature, treatments, materialized ficon_text, and the supersede chain pointer.
field_condition_thirdsThree rows per report (touchdown / midpoint / rollout): contaminant, depth, coverage, derived RwyCC, final RwyCC (possibly overridden), override flag, and override reason.

RwyCC derivation engine (lib/calculations/rwycc.ts)

deriveRwycc() is a pure function implementing AC 150/5200-30D Table 4-1. It maps a single third's (contaminant, depth, temperature) to a code from 6 (dry) down to 0 (wet ice / slush over ice). Key depth thresholds: wet ≤ 1/8 in → 5; wet > 1/8 in → 3 (or 2 for standing water); compacted snow is temperature-dependent (< −15 °C → 4, −15 to −3 °C → 3, > −3 °C → 2). The operator may override the derived value — the override requires a written reason stored in override_reason.

FICON NOTAM text (buildFiconNotamText())

The FICON text is materialized at INSERT time and stored in ficon_text. The format per AC 30D §6 is: RWY <id> <CC/CC/CC> <cov%/cov%/cov%> PCT <contaminant tokens>[<depth>IN][TRTD W/<treatments>]. Examples: RWY 13/31 5/5/5 100/100/100 PCT WET, RWY 06L/24R 3/3/2 80/100/100 PCT COMPACTED SN 2IN TRTD W/SAND. The depth token uses the maximum depth across all three thirds. Storing the text at INSERT (not recomputing on read) means the active-report card and history rows always show the exact NOTAM text that was live at the time.

UI workflow

The page shows one card per runway. When no active FCR exists, the card says 'conditions presumed dry (RwyCC 6/6/6)' per AC 30D. Issuing a new report or an 'Issue Update' opens a modal with a live FICON preview that updates as the operator types. On save, the FICON text is auto-copied to the clipboard so the operator can immediately paste it into the FAA NOTAM Manager.

code.caption: deriveRwycc — AC 150/5200-30D Table 4-1 RwyCC engine (pure function)
buildFiconNotamText — builds the FICON NOTAM body per AC 30D §6 from per-third data
createReport — full INSERT flow: derive RwyCC per third, materialize FICON text, insert report + thirds, back-fill superseded_by_id on the prior active report
code.from: export async function createReport(input: CreateReportInput): Promise<{

code.caption: fetchActiveByRunway — queries the non-superseded, still-valid report per runway using the superseded_by_id IS NULL + valid_until > now() partial index
code.from: export async function fetchActiveByRunway(baseId: string): Promise<FieldConditionReportWithThirds[]> {

fits: RwyCC engine: lib/calculations/rwycc.ts (pure, no Supabase imports). Data layer: lib/supabase/field-conditions.ts. Page: app/(app)/field-conditions/page.tsx. The page reads operating initials from the profiles table at mount time and stamps them on each report. The live FICON preview in the New Report modal is computed by calling buildFiconNotamText() client-side from the useMemo hook in NewReportModal, so the operator sees the exact NOTAM text before saving.

quiz.q: An airport operator issues an updated field condition report for the same runway. What happens to the previous report's superseded_by_id column?
choices: It remains NULL — only the valid_until timestamp is updated on the old row
It is set to the new report's UUID, making the old row inactive for the active-report query
The old row is deleted from field_condition_reports
A new status column is set to 'superseded' on the old row
quiz.explain: createReport() back-fills superseded_by_id on any prior active report for the same runway after inserting the new row. The active-report query in fetchActiveByRunway() filters for superseded_by_id IS NULL, so the old row is immediately hidden from the active view while remaining queryable in the history.

quiz.q: The operator observes compacted snow at -2°C on the touchdown third. What RwyCC does deriveRwycc() return?
choices: 4 — temperature is below -15°C
3 — temperature is between -15°C and -3°C
2 — temperature is above -3°C
1 — compacted snow always returns 1
quiz.explain: The compacted_snow case in deriveRwycc() is temperature-dependent: temperatureC < -15 → 4; temperatureC >= -15 AND < -3 → 3; temperatureC >= -3 → 2. At -2°C the temperature is above -3°C, so the code returns 2.

quiz.q: A field condition report's ficon_text is stored in the database at INSERT time rather than computed on read. What operational advantage does this provide?
choices: It allows the FICON text to be edited after the report is issued without creating a new superseding row
It ensures the active-report card and history rows always display the exact NOTAM text that was in effect at the time, even if the contaminant label strings later change
It reduces server load by avoiding the buildFiconNotamText() call on every page view
It enables full-text search on the FICON body in Supabase
quiz.explain: Materializing ficon_text at INSERT preserves the immutable audit record. If CONTAMINANT_NOTAM_TOKENS or TREATMENT_NOTAM_TOKENS constants ever change (e.g. a regulatory update), existing historical reports still show the exact NOTAM body that was live at the time. The server-load benefit is secondary; the primary reason is correctness of the historical record.
